features.name: Async HTTP Client
features.description: Fully async HTTP client built on httpcore with connection pooling and HTTP/2 support.

features.name: Streaming Responses
features.description: Memory-efficient streaming of large response bodies via async iteration and byte streaming.

features.name: HTTP/2 Support
features.description: Native HTTP/2 protocol support with multiplexed connections and header compression.

features.name: Transport API
features.description: Pluggable transport layer allowing custom backends for testing, mocking, and proxy support.

features.name: Timeout Configuration
features.description: Granular timeout controls for connect, read, write, and pool acquisition operations.

features.name: Event Hooks
features.description: Request and response lifecycle hooks for logging, metrics, and custom processing pipelines.

call_chain.role: Public async method that builds a Request object and dispatches it through the middleware pipeline.
Internal send method that handles redirects, auth flows, and delegates to the transport layer.
Sends a single request through the transport, handling connection acquisition and response construction.
Delegates to httpcore's async connection pool to perform the actual network I/O.
Constructs the Response object with lazy content decoding and stream management.

async_client.explanation: HTTPX's async client follows a layered architecture. AsyncClient.request() builds a Request model and sends it through _send() which handles redirect following and authentication flows. Each actual network call goes through _send_single_request() which delegates to the transport layer. The default AsyncHTTPTransport wraps httpcore's AsyncConnectionPool, which manages connection pooling, keep-alive, and HTTP/2 multiplexing. Responses are constructed lazily — the body isn't read until accessed, enabling efficient streaming of large payloads via aiter_bytes().

call_chain.role: Initializes the transport with connection pool configuration, SSL settings, and proxy parameters.
Protocol method that sends a request through httpcore and returns a response stream.
Test transport that returns pre-configured responses without making network calls.
Accepts a custom transport parameter, enabling users to swap the networking backend entirely.

transport_api.explanation: HTTPX's transport API defines a simple protocol: any class implementing handle_async_request(request) can serve as a transport backend. The default AsyncHTTPTransport wraps httpcore for real networking, but users can inject MockTransport for testing or build custom transports for proxy support, caching, or instrumentation. The AsyncClient accepts a transport parameter in its constructor, making the entire networking layer pluggable without modifying client logic.
